meta.title: LGBTQ+ Affirmative Therapy This Week | PhD/PsyD | Brooklyn Austin Miami
meta.description: LGBTQ+ affirmative therapy with a doctoral therapist this week. PhD/PsyD only, real coordinator match in 24 hours, $280-300/session. Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, online.

hero.h1: LGBTQ+ affirmative therapy this week, with a PhD or PsyD therapist.
hero.subhead: Whether you're working through identity, in a queer relationship, navigating a transition, or just dealing with the same things straight adults deal with, your first session can be this week. Every therapist holds a PhD or PsyD, and a real coordinator pairs you with the affirmative clinician who fits where you are on a five-minute call.
hero.trustLine: ✓ First session this week ✓ $280 to $300 per session ✓ Out-of-network claim filed for you
hero.imageAlt: Editorial portrait of a doctoral clinician in a WTG office

treat.h2: LGBTQ+ affirmative therapy is what we do, and the rest of the team is here when the work asks for more
treat.subhead: Six ways our doctoral team supports queer adults across identity, relationships, and the issues that don't have to do with being queer at all. Every clinician PhD or PsyD.

cards.title: Affirmative individual therapy
cards.body: Doctoral therapy for queer, trans, and non-binary adults. Identity is the focus when it should be, and not the focus when it shouldn't.

cards.title: LGBTQ+ couples therapy
cards.body: Affirmative couples work for same-sex, polyamorous, open, and any partnership that doesn't fit the default.

cards.title: Trans and gender-affirming care
cards.body: Doctoral support for clients exploring, transitioning, or post-transition.

cards.title: Coming out and identity work
cards.body: Whether you're exploring, just out, or 30 years out. Different stages need different work.

cards.title: Anxiety, depression, and trauma
cards.body: When queer adults come in for the same things straight adults do, with a clinician who gets the additional context.

cards.title: Psychiatry on the same affirming team
cards.body: Affirming psychiatrists when medication can help. One care plan, not two separate visits where you have to come out twice.

comparison.h2: How LGBTQ+ affirmative care at WTG compares to the rest of the field
comparison.subhead: What you'll get from most options versus what happens at WTG.

rows.them: You come out at the therapist's intake, then come out again at the psychiatrist, and a third time if you need couples work.
rows.us: Therapy, psychiatry, and couples work all on the same affirming team. Your story moves with you, with your consent. You come out once.

rows.them: Master's-level therapist with an 'LGBTQ-friendly' filter on Psychology Today.
rows.us: Every therapist holds a PhD or PsyD with documented affirmative training built into clinician development.

rows.them: A solo affirming therapist with a multi-week waitlist and no team to back them up.
rows.us: A multi-clinician doctoral team, if you need couples work, psychiatry, or a different fit, we have it on the same care plan.

rows.them: You scroll a directory and pick a name based on whether they look queer in the headshot.
rows.us: A real coordinator listens to what you're working through and picks the affirmative therapist whose lens fits you, on a five-minute call.

rows.them: Out-of-network paperwork dumped on you to figure out alone.
rows.us: Reimbursify files your OON claim for you, and most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps from your first call to your first session
howItWorks.subhead: Under five minutes to start. No long forms, no chatbots, a real person answers and asks three questions about what you're working through.

steps.title: Call or tap to book
steps.body: Reach our patient coordinator by phone or online. A real person gets back to you the same day, on a five-minute call.

steps.title: Get matched in 24 hours
steps.body: We pair you with an affirmative doctoral therapist whose practice area, lens, and style fit what you're working through. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

steps.title: First session
steps.body: Meet in person or over secure video, often within the same week.

steps.title: Make progress
steps.body: Concrete tools and check-ins so you can see the change in your day-to-day, not just in session.

whyWTG.h2: Five reasons LGBTQ+ adults choose WTG over a solo affirming therapist
whyWTG.subhead: Where doctoral training meets actual affirming care across the full team.

bullets.title: You don't come out twice
bullets.body: Therapy, psychiatry, and couples work all on the same affirming team. Your story moves with your consent. No retelling, no re-disclosing, no starting over.

bullets.title: Only doctoral-level therapists
bullets.body: PhD and PsyD only, with documented LGBTQ+ affirmative training. Most practices stop at 'LGBTQ-friendly' as a filter checkbox. We don't.

bullets.title: Real coordinator match in 24 hours
bullets.body: Tell our coordinator what matters most in the match, clinician with experience working with trans clients, polyamorous relationships, religious deconstruction, intersectional identities. Our team is large enough to match specifically rather than generally.

bullets.title: Couples and family work on the same team
bullets.body: Same-sex, poly, open, chosen-family. Your relationships get the same affirmative doctoral standard.

bullets.title: Multi-clinician backup
bullets.body: Solo affirming practitioners book up. If your matched therapist is out, full, or eventually doesn't fit, we have a team on the same affirming standard. You're not stuck.

clinicians.eyebrow: Meet a few of our therapists
clinicians.h2: Doctoral affirmative specialists across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online
clinicians.subhead: Our coordinator can pair you with any doctoral therapist on the team trained in affirmative care. The four below are placeholder slots for real WTG affirmative specialists; final names and headshots replace these before launch.

midCTA.body: Same-week first sessions for LGBTQ+ affirmative therapy across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online. Whoever you are, we hold space.

pricing.subhead: We're a cash-pay practice. No insurer dictating how many sessions you get, no diagnosis code filed to your permanent medical record.
pricing.reimbursement: Many patients have out-of-network benefits. We partner with Reimbursify, an independent service that submits your out-of-network claim to your insurer for you. Most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.
steps: We charge you the session fee at the time of service.
We send you a superbill you can submit to your insurance.
Reimbursify files the claim for you. You get reimbursed directly.

reviews.quote: Dr. Selling and the team at WTG are the best! Beautiful office space in Brooklyn.
reviews.author: Michael F.

reviews.quote: I highly recommend Dr. Trubowitz, she's helped our family resolve some longstanding issues. This is the best group of doctors in Austin that I've worked with. The space is also beautiful and relaxing.
reviews.author: Rebecca B.

reviews.quote: This office is unlike any other therapy office I've ever seen. Totally clean, beautiful curved wood everywhere, complimentary tea and water. Particularly impressed with the group therapy room. Just a really nice place to be.
reviews.author: Jake F.

faq.h2: Everything LGBTQ+ patients ask before the first session

items.q: How much does therapy cost, and how long will it take?
items.a: Sessions are $280 to $300 each. Most adults meet weekly to start, often shifting to every other week as the work progresses. Patients doing focused work often see meaningful change in 12 to 20 sessions. Patients doing deeper identity, relational, or trauma work may stay longer. Your therapist will set goals with you in the first sessions and check progress regularly. We're not in the business of keeping you in therapy forever.

items.q: How do I know your therapists are actually affirming?
items.a: Affirmative training is a documented part of our clinician development standard, not just a self-described filter. Our doctoral therapists complete continuing education in LGBTQ+ affirmative care, and our coordinator can tell you specifically what training a particular therapist has done. You're also welcome to ask the therapist directly in your first session. Affirmative therapists welcome that question.

items.q: How will I know if my therapist understands my specific experience?
items.a: Tell your coordinator what matters most in the match, whether that's a clinician with experience working with trans clients specifically, with polyamorous relationships, with religious deconstruction, with intersectional identities, or any other lens. Our team is large enough to match you specifically rather than generally.

items.q: Can you write letters for gender-affirming care?
items.a: Some of our clinicians write letters for gender-affirming medical care when clinically appropriate, and your coordinator can match you specifically with a clinician who does. If letter-writing is a key part of why you're reaching out, mention it during your first call so we pair you correctly.

items.q: Do you take my insurance for LGBTQ+ therapy?
items.a: We're a cash-pay practice, so we don't bill insurance directly. Most patients use out-of-network benefits and get reimbursed 50 to 80 percent of the session fee. We give you a superbill and partner with Reimbursify, a claim-filing service, that submits it to your insurer for you.

form.eyebrow: Ready to take the next step?
form.h2: Tell us a little about what you're looking for
form.subhead: Our patient coordinator will call or email you back the same day. No waitlists, no chatbots, no pressure. The first session can be this week.
form.consent: By leaving your phone or email, you are consenting to receiving communications from us via text or email and that may include communications about your appointments, services, and marketing information related to our services. You may opt out of marketing information at any time. Your contact information will never be sold to any third party and will only be shared to the extent necessary to perform any services on your behalf.
form.phoneFallback: or call 786-692-6547 to speak with our coordinator now.
form.reassurance: Same-day response. No waitlists, no runaround.
